_comment: Demo 2 — Painted-Blur. Skeleton for the 2D-field-as-parameter demo. Requires source.mp4 (~5s clip with clear foreground/background separation — e.g., a person walking, a flower in a field) + initial-mask.png (1920x1080 grayscale, circular dark gradient over foreground area). The blur effect's `radius` param accepts EITHER scalar OR field reference (per SPEC-4 §3.3). Renderer samples field at (y, x) per pixel and uses the value as blur radius.
name: Painted-Blur
description: Blur isn't one number — it's a painted field. Where the mask is dark, the frame is sharp. Light = blurred.

params._comment_radius: Field-typed param: renderer samples this grayscale image at (y, x) per pixel for the radius value. Range: 0 (black) → 32px (white). User can edit via in-app paint tool (SPEC-4 §3.4).

demoMetadata.label: Blur isn't just one number anymore. Paint where it's sharp.
demoMetadata.interactionInstructions: Paint over the foreground to keep it sharp. Erase to blur. The field updates the frame live.
